title: Add 'Structural Analogy Audit' skill for analyst and writer agents
description: Add a learned skill that, whenever Mira reasons by cross-market or cross-cultural analogy ('X is the Y of Z'), forces explicit enumeration of the structural conditions that make Y work, then checks each condition for transfer. The skill produces a flagged list: conditions that transfer, conditions that don't, and a verdict on whether the analogy describes a mechanism or just a functional surface.
rationale: The reading note's core insight is that tech analogies routinely conflate functional vision with generative mechanism — Palantir's moat is an embedded-engineer contract structure that has no Chinese institutional equivalent, not the software itself. Mira's analyst agent produces market predictions and her writer agent uses analogies as rhetorical scaffolding. Both are exposed to exactly this failure mode: 'AI will do to X what Palantir did to defense' sounds valid until you enumerate the conditions (long-duration government contracts, on-site embedding rights, classified data handling clearance) and find they don't transfer. The note also flags a second failure: systematic blind spots arise when analysts lack a reference object for a foreign model — meaning the check should run in both directions (over-analogizing AND under-recognizing novel structures).
diff_description: New skill file `structural_analogy_audit.md` with three steps: (1) Decompose — list 3–5 structural conditions (institutional, contractual, regulatory, incentive) that make the source analogue work, not just what it does; (2) Transfer check — for each condition, explicitly state whether it exists in the target context and why; (3) Verdict — 'mechanism transfer' (all conditions present), 'surface analogy' (functional similarity only, structural gap flagged), or 'blind spot risk' (target has no reference object in source context, systematic underestimation likely). In `analyst/prompts.py` and `writer/prompts.py`, add a one-line instruction: 'When drawing a cross-market or cross-cultural analogy, apply the Structural Analogy Audit skill before committing to the comparison.' No logic changes, no data flow changes — prompt addition only.
